faq_articles.title: Insurance Claims for Roof Damage
faq_articles.content:
If your roof has been damaged by hail, wind, falling debris, or other covered events, you may be able to file a homeowners insurance claim. Start by documenting the damage with clear photos, then schedule a professional inspection to create a written report and estimate. Contact your insurer as soon as possible to open a claim and provide them with our inspection report and photos. During the adjuster visit, you can request that our project manager be present to answer technical questions. Once the claim is approved, we will help you choose materials, review your scope of loss, and coordinate repairs or full roof replacement in compliance with your policy.

Key steps:
1. Ensure safety and stop active leaks.
2. Document visible damage and temporary repairs.
3. Schedule an inspection with our team.
4. File a claim with your insurer and share our report.
5. Review the adjuster estimate and scope of work.
6. Approve final materials and schedule the project.

If you need help at any stage, use the "Request Insurance Assistance" form and our team will walk you through the process.

faq_articles.title: What to Do If Your Roof Is Actively Leaking
faq_articles.content: An active roof leak requires quick action to limit interior damage. First, move furniture and belongings away from the leak and place buckets or containers under any dripping water. Do not attempt to get on the roof during a storm or at night. Instead, call our 24/7 emergency repair line or submit the "Request Emergency Service" form on our Emergency Repair page. We prioritize same-day or within-24-hours appointments for active leaks. Our technician will locate the source, perform a temporary repair if needed, and provide recommendations for permanent fixes or full replacement.

faq_articles.title: How to Schedule a Roof Inspection Online
faq_articles.content: You can schedule a roof inspection online in just a few steps. From our homepage, click the "Schedule Inspection" button. Enter your ZIP code to see available dates and time windows in your area, then choose your property type (residential, commercial, or multi-family) and the main reason for the inspection, such as leak or water damage, storm damage, or annual maintenance. Next, use the date picker to move to the week you prefer and filter by morning, afternoon, or full-day availability. Select an open time slot and confirm your contact details to book the appointment. You will receive an email confirmation and reminders before the visit.

faq_articles.title: Roof Replacement: What to Expect
faq_articles.content: A full roof replacement typically takes 1–3 days for most residential homes, depending on size, complexity, and weather. Our crew will protect landscaping, remove old roofing down to the decking, inspect for wood damage, and install underlayment, flashings, and your chosen roofing material. We perform a thorough cleanup at the end of each day, including nails and debris. Before your project starts, we provide a written scope of work, material selection summary, and warranty details. During the project, your project manager will be your main point of contact for questions, change orders, and inspection scheduling.

faq_articles.title: Asphalt Shingles vs. Metal Roofing
faq_articles.content: Asphalt shingles and metal roofing are two of the most popular options for residential roofs. Asphalt shingles typically offer a lower upfront cost and a wide variety of styles and colors, with warranties often ranging from 20–30 years. Metal roofing usually costs more initially but provides superior durability, energy efficiency, and longer warranties, often 35–50 years. When comparing options, consider total lifespan, warranty coverage, curb appeal, and noise preferences. Our Materials page provides warranty and starting price details for both options, and you can click "Select for My Project" on either material to save your preference.

faq_articles.title: Roofing Project Financing Options
faq_articles.content: We partner with multiple financing providers to offer flexible payment options for your roofing project. Common terms include 24, 36, 48, 60, and 84-month plans, with competitive APRs and no prepayment penalties on most options. You can use our Financing Calculator to estimate monthly payments—simply enter your project cost, then compare terms to find a payment that fits your budget. After choosing a plan, submit a quick online application with basic personal and contact information. Approval decisions are often returned within minutes, allowing you to move forward with your roof replacement, repair, or maintenance plan without delay.

faq_articles.title: Using the Roofing Financing Calculator
faq_articles.content: Our financing calculator helps estimate your monthly payment for a roof replacement, repair, or maintenance project. Enter your total project cost, then choose from the available term lengths listed in months. The calculator will display an estimated monthly payment for each term, based on the selected provider's APR and and standard assumptions. To minimize your monthly payment, choose the longest term with a payment that fits your budget. Once you select a term, click "Select This Option" or "Choose Plan" to begin the application process.

faq_articles.title: Do I Need a Roof Maintenance Plan?
faq_articles.content: Routine roof maintenance can extend the life of your roofing system, reduce the risk of leaks, and protect your warranty. Our maintenance plans typically include scheduled inspections, minor sealant touch-ups, debris removal from roofing surfaces, and photo documentation. Residential customers often choose 1–2 visits per year, depending on tree coverage and local weather. In our Maintenance Plans section, you can use the "Build Your Plan" tool to set your property type, roof size, and visit frequency, then optionally add services like gutter cleaning or skylight inspection.

faq_articles.title: Understanding Roofing Warranties
faq_articles.content: Roofing warranties typically include both manufacturer and workmanship coverage. Manufacturer warranties cover defects in roofing materials for a specified number of years, while workmanship warranties cover installation-related issues. Asphalt shingles and metal roofing offer different warranty periods—our Materials pages list warranty years and expected lifespan for each option. Keep your warranty valid by following maintenance guidelines and addressing leaks promptly. Always store copies of your contract, material specifications, and warranty registration documents.

faq_articles.title: Current Roof Replacement Promotions
faq_articles.content: We regularly offer seasonal promotions on full roof replacement projects, including percentage discounts, fixed-dollar savings, and free add-ons like gutter cleaning. Visit our Special Offers page to view current deals and filter by service type. Each promotion card lists the discount amount, eligible services, minimum project cost, and expiration date. Click "View Details" on a promotion to see full terms and, when you are ready, click "Apply to My Project" to attach that offer to a new quote request.

faq_articles.title: Our Service Areas
faq_articles.content: We provide roofing services across a wide metro area, including residential and commercial properties in dozens of ZIP codes. To confirm availability for your address, enter your ZIP code on the Schedule Inspection page or request a quote. For emergency repairs, we prioritize calls within our core service zones to ensure a rapid response.

faq_articles.title: How Insurance Deductibles Work for Roof Claims
faq_articles.content: Your insurance deductible is the amount you are responsible for paying before your homeowners insurance covers the remaining approved cost of a roof claim. For example, if your policy has a $1,000 deductible and the insurer approves $10,000 in roof replacement costs, the insurer typically pays $9,000 and you pay $1,000. Some policies have separate wind or hail deductibles that are a percentage of your dwelling limit. Review your policy or speak with your agent to confirm your deductible amounts before approving work.

faq_articles.title: How to Request a Roof Replacement Quote
faq_articles.content: You can request a roof replacement quote by visiting our Services section and selecting Roof Replacement. Click the "Request a Quote" button to open the quote form. Provide your property type (such as residential), basic project details, desired roofing material (for example, asphalt shingles or metal roofing), budget range, and contact information. Including accurate address and ZIP code information helps us confirm service availability and approximate travel time. Once submitted, our team will review your request and contact you to schedule an on-site visit or remote estimate, depending on the project.

promotions.name: 15% Off Full Roof Replacement
promotions.description: Save 15% on labor and materials for a full roof replacement project when your contract is signed before the promotion end date.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: Valid for full roof replacement projects only. Minimum project cost $8,000 before discount. Cannot be combined with other percentage-off offers. Contract must be signed by 04/30/2026.

promotions.name: 10% Off Roof Replacement for New Customers
promotions.description: New customers receive 10% off their first full roof replacement project.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: Offer valid for first-time customers only. Full roof replacement projects with a minimum value of $6,000 qualify. Cannot be combined with ROOF15 or other percentage discounts.

promotions.name: $750 Off Premium Roof Replacement
promotions.description: Get $750 off premium material roof replacement projects, including metal roofing and synthetic slate.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: Valid on premium material upgrades only, including metal and synthetic systems. Minimum project cost $12,000. May be combined with select financing offers only.

promotions.name: Spring Roof Tune-Up Special
promotions.description: Receive one complimentary maintenance visit when you sign up for a new annual residential maintenance plan.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: New maintenance plan customers only. Complimentary visit must be used within 12 months of enrollment. Not valid with other maintenance discounts.

promotions.name: Emergency Trip Fee Waived with Repair
promotions.description: We waive the standard emergency trip fee when you authorize recommended leak repairs during the initial visit.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: Trip fee waiver applies only when recommended repairs are completed during the same visit. Not valid for inspection-only appointments.

promotions.name: $99 Storm Damage Inspection
promotions.description: Post-storm inspection for visible roof damage at a flat $99 fee, credited back if you proceed with approved repairs or replacement.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: Limited-time promotional inspection rate. Inspection fee is applied as a credit if repairs or replacement are completed within 90 days.

promotions.name: 5% Off Roof Replacement with Maintenance Plan
promotions.description: Save 5% on a full roof replacement when you bundle with a 2-year maintenance plan.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: Maintenance plan must be purchased together with the roof replacement contract to receive the bundled discount.

promotions.name: Reduced APR on Roof Financing
promotions.description: Qualifying customers receive a reduced APR on select 36–60 month financing plans for roof replacement projects.
promotions.terms_and_conditions: Subject to credit approval. APR reduction applies only to participating financing programs and may not be combined with other financing promotions.

reviews.title: Smooth roof replacement and great cleanup
reviews.content: Our 2-story home needed a full roof replacement after years of wear. The crew arrived on time, protected our landscaping, and finished in two days. We chose architectural asphalt shingles and the house looks brand new. They cleaned up nails and debris thoroughly.

reviews.title: Metal roof upgrade was worth it
reviews.content: We upgraded from old shingles to a standing seam metal roof. The project manager explained everything clearly and helped coordinate with our HOA. The metal roof is quieter than expected and looks sharp. Pricing was fair and they stuck to the schedule.

reviews.title: Professional crew and clear communication
reviews.content: From the initial inspection to the final walkthrough, the team was professional and responsive. They sent photos during the tear-off and showed us a few damaged boards they replaced. Our new asphalt shingle roof came in under the budget we shared on the quote form.

reviews.title: Fast replacement after hail damage
reviews.content: Our roof was badly damaged in a hail storm. The company helped with the insurance claim and started the replacement as soon as it was approved. Work was completed in two days and the yard was spotless after.

reviews.title: New tile roof on duplex
reviews.content: We replaced the aging roof on our small duplex with concrete tile. The estimate was transparent, and the crew took extra care around tenant entrances. The new roof looks great and feels solid.

reviews.title: Good work but minor scheduling delays
reviews.content: Happy with the finished roof, but weather delays pushed the project back by a few days. The team kept us updated, and the final result is excellent.

reviews.title: Emergency leak repair on a rainy night
reviews.content: They answered the phone late at night and had a tech out within a few hours. He stopped the leak and explained the follow-up work we needed. Very grateful.

reviews.title: Flashing repair solved persistent leak
reviews.content: After another company failed to solve our chimney leak, this team identified flashing issues and fixed them on the first visit.

reviews.title: Maintenance plan gives peace of mind
reviews.content: We signed up for twice-a-year roof maintenance for our 2,300 sq ft home. The techs are thorough and always share photos of what they inspected. Highly recommend the program.

reviews.title: Clear inspection report helped with insurance
reviews.content: The inspector arrived on time, took detailed photos, and explained all the hail damage they found. Their report made it easy to file an insurance claim.

reviews.title: Helpful insurance assistance team
reviews.content: I was overwhelmed by the insurance process after storm damage. Their team walked me through each step, reviewed my adjuster’s estimate, and made sure my roof was fully covered.

reviews.title: Financing made a big project manageable
reviews.content: We used their 60-month financing option for a $18k roof replacement. The online application was quick and we got an answer in minutes. Monthly payments fit our budget.

reviews.title: Good value for commercial maintenance
reviews.content: We have a flat TPO roof on a small warehouse and the maintenance plan has caught minor issues before they became big leaks.

reviews.title: Older roof replacement project
reviews.content: Three years later, our roof still looks great and we haven’t had a single leak. Glad we went with this company.

roofing_materials.name: Architectural Asphalt Shingles
roofing_materials.description: Durable laminated asphalt shingles suitable for most residential roofs. Offered in multiple colors and profiles to match neighborhood guidelines and personal style.
pros: Wide range of colors and styles
Good balance of cost and durability
cons: Shorter lifespan than metal or tile
Can be more susceptible to hail and wind damage
May require more frequent replacement in harsh climates

roofing_materials.name: Standing Seam Metal Roofing
roofing_materials.description: High-performance standing seam metal panels with concealed fasteners for a clean look and long-term durability. Ideal for homeowners seeking long-lasting protection and modern curb appeal.
pros: Long service life and extended warranties
Excellent resistance to wind, hail, and fire
Reflective finishes can improve energy efficiency
cons: Higher upfront cost than asphalt shingles
Longer lead times for some colors or profiles

roofing_materials.name: Clay & Concrete Tile Roofing
roofing_materials.description: Classic clay and concrete tile systems that provide a distinctive look and exceptional durability for the right structures.
pros: High curb appeal in many architectural styles
cons: Heavier than other materials; structural review required

roofing_materials.name: TPO Single-Ply Membrane
roofing_materials.description: Energy-efficient TPO single-ply membrane system for low-slope and flat roofs, commonly used on commercial buildings and modern residential additions.
pros: Reflective white surface helps reduce cooling loads
cons: Foot traffic can damage membrane without walk pads

roofing_materials.name: Synthetic Slate Shingles
roofing_materials.description: Lightweight synthetic shingles that mimic the look of natural slate without the weight and cost.
cons: Requires certified installers to maintain warranty
